root_motivation: Software work is silent and visually homogeneous, so days bleed together. A generative ambient piece driven by jsy's laptop telemetry (file saves, builds, idle, focus, fragmentation) gives the workday auditory texture and yields a daily .wav archive — an audible diary. This crate is the orchestrator slice (PRD Phase 1): a Rust binary that ingests an NDJSON telemetry stream and emits NDJSON parameter-bus cues for an external audio engine (Sonic Pi / SuperCollider). The engine, sample library, .wav recorder, and annual album ritual are explicit non-goals here.
user_persona: jsy at the laptop during work hours, piping a real or synthetic NDJSON telemetry stream (from ctrace, build hooks, or test fixtures) into `ambient` and routing its NDJSON cue output to a separate audio-engine bridge process. Sole operator; no multi-user surface.

acceptance_criteria.description: Reads newline-delimited JSON telemetry events from stdin and writes newline-delimited JSON cue events to stdout, one cue per accepted input event (subject to throttle/silence rules).

Recognizes the seven PRD §3 event kinds — file_save, file_create, build_pass, build_fail, idle, high_focus, fragmentation — and maps each to a distinct voice (chime, piano, settle, grain, silence, drift, poly respectively) in the emitted cue.

Throttles each voice with a per-voice minimum gap so a burst of incoming events of the same kind does not produce a burst of cues; defaults (seconds): chime 4, piano 8, settle 30, grain 15, drift 120, poly 30, silence 300. Suppressed events are dropped silently (no cue line emitted).
Malformed input lines (invalid JSON, unknown kind, missing required field) are skipped without aborting the run; the process continues consuming subsequent lines and exits 0 on EOF.
Per-voice throttle gap is configurable via CLI flags (e.g. --chime-secs, --piano-secs, --settle-secs, --grain-secs, --drift-secs, --poly-secs, --silence-secs); flags override the defaults.
Emitted cue lines are valid JSON objects carrying at minimum {voice, kind, unix} and pass `serde_json::from_str::<Value>` round-trip.

high_focus and fragmentation cues carry an intensity-like numeric field derived from the event payload (run_seconds for high_focus, switch_count for fragmentation), so the downstream engine can scale drift slope / polyrhythmic density.
The cli accepts --help and --version and exits 0; --help lists every voice override flag.

scope: Rust CLI binary `ambient` plus a small library crate exposing the parse/map/throttle pipeline.
NDJSON ingest from stdin; NDJSON cue emit to stdout; one-pass streaming.
Seven event-kind → voice mappings per PRD §3.
Resilient parsing: malformed lines logged to stderr (or silently dropped) but never abort the run.
Unit + integration test suite covering the AC table.
non_goals: Audio synthesis or sample playback (no Sonic Pi / SuperCollider / OSC client in this crate).
Daily .wav recording or annual album mastering.
Foreground music: no melodies, no songs, no drums.
Live remix UI / web dashboard / TUI.
Distribution to anyone beyond jsy + K + M.

five_whys_trace.a: Because PRD-ambient-compositions wants a generative piece driven by laptop signals; the orchestrator is the gluing piece that turns telemetry into engine parameters.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a Rust orchestrator rather than scripting it directly inside Sonic Pi?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the telemetry layer (ctrace, wchg, builds) already lives in Rust/CLI land; a Rust binary stays close to that source, debounces and throttles deterministically, and is testable in CI without an audio engine running.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a one-pass NDJSON in / NDJSON out pipeline instead of OSC sockets in v1?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the PRD's risk catalog flags 'distraction' and 'default ugliness'; keeping the orchestrator a pure stdio transducer makes it trivially testable, scriptable, and replayable from archived event logs without binding to a live audio engine.

five_whys_trace.q: Why throttle per voice with a minimum gap rather than batching or smoothing?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the failure mode the PRD fears is 'meditation-app texture': a save-storm becoming a chime-storm. A min-gap throttle directly addresses the burst-to-burst transduction and is easy to reason about in tests.

five_whys_trace.q: Why expose every voice's throttle gap as a CLI flag instead of a config file?
five_whys_trace.a: Because jsy will iterate on these numbers against taste over months; flag overrides keep iteration cheap (rerun a fixture with `--chime-secs 8`) without a config-format dependency.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does this crate also drive the audio engine?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No — PRD §4 splits orchestrator from engine; this crate ends at NDJSON cue stdout. The engine is an external process.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What 'voice' label do we use for each PRD event kind?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Adopted the seven-voice table from the prior hand-built README (chime/piano/settle/grain/silence/drift/poly) since it faithfully expresses the PRD §3 prose.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What are the default throttle seconds?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: chime 4, piano 8, settle 30, grain 15, drift 120, poly 30, silence 300 — carried forward from the hand-built defaults as a reasonable starting point; jsy will tune via CLI flags.
